ELPAC Level 4
Lexile BR–300L
DTR · TALK ABOUT A SCENE

One Inch Under Oath

STS-MS7-L4-BR300-MSMATH7-MKT-AAD-01 • MS Math Grade 7 • Scale Drawings
Look at the picture. You will answer some questions about it. Press the play button to hear each question. Record your answer for each question. You can listen to your answer and record it again. Click SUBMIT RECORDINGS when you have answered all the questions.
JUST FOR FUN · OPTIONAL
Long before the first brush touched the wall, the neighborhood planned the Freedom Wall mural on paper, one small drawing standing in for a wall taller than a house. Artists used a scale so every hand and every color would land in the right place, and the whole community could vote on the design before a single ladder went up. On this blueprint, the rule is 1 inch = 4 feet.
If the wall on the blueprint measures 6 inches tall, how tall is the real wall?
Optional. You do not have to record this one.
1
What is Amara doing?
Look at the picture. Say: Amara is ____. I know because I see her ____. (pointing • writing) (finger • foot)
2
What is Kwame holding?
Look at the picture. Say: Kwame is holding ____. I know because it has ____. (a ruler • a book) (marks • pages)
3
What is the teacher doing?
Look at the picture. Say: The teacher ____ the exhibit. I know because I see the ____. (stamps • hides) (red mark • door)
4
What room is this? How do you know?
Look at the picture. Say: This is a ____ room. I know because I see the ____. (court • art) (scales • paint)
5
Tell me about Exhibit A on the easel.
Look at the picture. Say: Exhibit A is a ____. I know because it has ____. (drawing • photo) (a scale • a frame)
6
What is Zuri wearing?
Look at the picture. Say: Zuri wears a ____ shirt. I know because I see the ____. (yellow • blue) (color • clock)
Record an answer for each question, then submit.
Content alignment: CCSS 7.G.A.1 — Scale Drawings. ELD (Speaking — official ELPAC “Talk About a Scene”): PI.A.1, PII.B.3, PII.B.4, PII.B.5.
